entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop studied, specifically Arachis hypogaea

entities.name: SOWING DEPTH
entities.description: The depth at which seeds are planted, categorized as shallow or deep

entities.name: HYPOCOTYL GROWTH
entities.description: The length and mass of the hypocotyl

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The mass and development of the root system

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The total yield of pods produced by the groundnut plant

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The formation of nodules on the hypocotyl and roots

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The experimental approach used to observe the effects of sowing depth

entities.name: VIRGINIA TYPES
entities.description: Specific varieties of groundnut that show different nodulation patterns

entities.name: NITROGENASE ACTIVITY
entities.description: The enzymatic activity responsible for nitrogen fixation

relationships.description: Deep sowing increases hypocotyl mass and length is a function of depth
Deep sowing results in decreased pod yields
Deep sowing increases hypocotyl nodules but decreases root nodules
Deep sowing leads to a loss in nitrogenase activity
Sowing depth is an experimental variable applied to groundnut
Field experiments were used to test the effect of sowing depth
Virginia types are a specific variety of groundnut
Nodules are the sites where nitrogen fixation occurs
Nitrogenase activity is the mechanism for nitrogen fixation
